### Post by WanneBeAHippy on 2010-04-05
Hey guys

i need help.

Since hours i try to mount my former windows HDD d: .
There are many important files.

my Desktop has 2 HDD 
80 gb + 164 gb

> # /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=****** / ext3 relatime,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda5 :
UUID=****** none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto,exec,utf8 0 0

/dev/fd0 /media/floppy0 auto rw,user,noauto,exec,utf8 0 0
/dev/sdb1 /mnt/zweitePlatte ntfs-3g ro 0 1The 80 GB HDD is found as sda and mounted as u see.

hope u can help me
wannebe

### Post by Morbius1 on 2010-04-11
**EXAMPLE:**[I]

STEP 1: Find out how the system sees your partitions[/I]

Open **Terminal**
Type **sudo blkid -c /dev/null**

This will output , among other things the following partition that I want to mount at boot:

/dev/sdb1: UUID="DA9056C19056A3B3" LABEL="WinXP" TYPE="ntfs"
[COLOR=Blue]*This is just to verify that sdb1 is in fact an NTFS partition.*[/COLOR]

*STEP 2: Create a mount point for your partition to live in*

Open **Terminal**
Type **sudo mkdir /mnt/zweitePlatte**

*STEP 3: Add a line to /etc/fstab/ to have this partitions mount at boot:*

> /dev/sdb1 /mnt/zweitePlatte ntfs defaults,nls=utf8,umask=007,gid=46 0 0*STEP 4: Comment out the following line in fstab:*
>  /dev/sdb1 /mnt/zweitePlatte ntfs-3g ro 0 1                      By adding a "#"sign in front of it so that it looks like this:
>  #/dev/sdb1 /mnt/zweitePlatte ntfs-3g ro 0 1                      *STEP 5: Save fstab and execute the new fstab:*

Save the edited fstab then:
Open **Terminal**
Type [B]sudo mount -a

umask=007 [/B]will allow owner ( which will be root ) and group ( which will be plugdev ) to read and write to that partition.
[B]
gid=46 [/B]will make the group 46, which is plugdev, and all local login users by default are members of the plugdev group.
